note:
Zero HOT setups? No WARM setups? You gotta be kidding me! That’s like walking into a bakery and finding out they’re outta bread. But guess what? The market is telling us something. Are we listening? The squeeze tracker is a barometer for speculation—and when it’s bone dry like this, it’s hard to miss the message: **risk appetite is cooling off, full stop.** This doesn’t mean the game is over, but it does mean we adjust our expectations. You don’t paddle upstream in a dry river; you pivot. 

And yet, the major indexes couldn’t care less. $SPY and $QQQ are hanging tough near their highs while $IWM is doing its usual small-cap shuffle, trying to figure out who it wants to be when it grows up. Meanwhile, bonds are stuck in purgatory with $TLT grinding sideways, and the dollar refuses to roll over. The macro picture is screaming “wait and see,” and here’s your cheat code: **when there’s no tailwind from aggressive risk appetite, you don’t push; you let the setups come to you.**

Speaking of setups, did you see what’s going on in the portfolio? Folks, we’ve got some monsters quietly crushing it. $UMAC still holding the crown with a staggering 1,357% max gain on one tranche, followed by another at 679%. That has leadership written all over it. And if drones are dominating, you can bet global defense and aerospace narratives are alive and well. Keep the radar on $ONDS and $SMR, which continue to outperform.

On the flip side, $GME is back in meme territory, up 50% since our entry. If you’re not trimming into these pops, what are you even doing? $COIN? Up 140%. The crypto complex is still showing signs of life—even without new HOT squeezes lighting up the board, our exposure here with $BMNR, $ETHA, $BLSH, and $SBET is paying the bills.

For those who missed the initial waves, here’s where you might still get in: $U is pushing that 90% gain and looks like it’s got room to run. That’s not just speculation; that’s a trade with momentum and a story behind it. $IOVA is up 72% and showing no signs of slowing, as biotech creeps back into investors’ radars. And $LI, with China trying to rev the EV engines again, also worth a second look for fresh entries.

For today, watch the dollar. With no fresh squeezes and a neutral regime dominating across sectors, this consolidation phase won’t last forever. A major breakout—or breakdown—in $DXY could set the tone for the next move. Also, keep an eye on $SMR and $UEC; uranium plays are quietly looking like they might want another leg higher. The bears? They’ve got nothing. Stress test your portfolio today, raise stops, and remember: the calm always precedes the storm. Are you ready to flip the switch?
